articles.title: Build with Lyria 3, our newest music generation model
articles.summary: Lyria 3 is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and for testing in Google AI Studio. This represents Google's latest advancement in AI music generation technology.

articles.title: Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more Google products
articles.summary: Google is bringing Lyria 3 to professional tools and workspaces where creators work daily. The Pro version offers enhanced capabilities for generating longer, more customizable music tracks.

articles.title: Mistral releases a new open-source model for speech generation
articles.summary: Mistral's new speech model can run on a smartwatch or smartphone. This represents a significant advancement in making speech generation accessible on edge devices.

articles.title: Cohere launches an open-source voice model specifically for transcription
articles.summary: The model is relatively lightweight at just 2 billion parameters and supports 14 languages. It's designed for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host transcription services.

articles.title: Updates to GitHub Copilot interaction data usage policy
articles.summary: From April 24 onward, interaction data from Copilot Free, Pro, and Pro+ users will be used to train AI models unless users opt out. This policy change affects inputs, outputs, code snippets, and associated context data.

articles.title: OpenAI shelves erotic chatbot 'indefinitely'
articles.summary: OpenAI has paused plans to release a sexualized "adult mode" for ChatGPT due to pushback from employees and investors. This decision comes alongside the discontinuation of their text-to-video AI platform Sora.

articles.title: Harvey confirms $11B valuation: Sequoia triples down
articles.summary: AI legal tech startup Harvey has secured significant investment from major firms including Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz, and Kleiner Perkins. The $11B valuation reflects strong investor confidence in AI applications for legal services.

articles.title: Granola raises $125M, hits $1.5B valuation as it expands from meeting notetaker to enterprise AI app
articles.summary: Granola's valuation jumped from $250 million to $1.5 billion with this funding round. The company is expanding beyond meeting notes to broader enterprise AI applications and has added more support for AI agents.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
articles.summary: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced legislation to halt new data center construction until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation. This reflects growing concerns about AI infrastructure and energy consumption.

articles.title: EU backs nude app ban and delays to landmark AI rules
articles.summary: European lawmakers voted to delay key parts of the EU AI Act while backing proposals to ban nudify apps. Compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems have been pushed back to December 2027.

articles.title: Internal Safety Collapse in Frontier Large Language Models
articles.summary: Researchers identify Internal Safety Collapse (ISC), where models continuously generate harmful content while executing benign tasks. The study introduces a framework that triggers ISC through domain tasks where harmful content becomes the only valid completion.

articles.title: MSA: Memory Sparse Attention for Efficient End-to-End Memory Model Scaling to 100M Tokens
articles.summary: This paper presents a memory-efficient attention mechanism that enables LLMs to process up to 100 million tokens. The approach addresses the fundamental constraint of full-attention architectures in handling lifetime-scale information.

articles.title: The Diminishing Returns of Early-Exit Decoding in Modern LLMs
articles.summary: Research shows that early-exit strategies for reducing LLM inference costs are less effective in modern models. Recent LLMs have improved architectures that reduce layer redundancy, limiting early-exit opportunities.

articles.title: Self-Distillation for Multi-Token Prediction
articles.summary: The paper proposes MTP-D, a self-distillation method for improving multi-token prediction in LLMs. This approach addresses challenges in acceptance rates and joint training of multiple prediction heads to accelerate inference.

articles.title: LiteLLM Hack: Were You One of the 47,000?
articles.summary: Simon Willison discusses a security incident affecting the LiteLLM project that impacted 47,000 users. This highlights important security considerations for AI development tools and infrastructure.

articles.title: Thoughts on slowing the fuck down
articles.summary: A reflective piece from Simon Willison on the pace of development in AI and technology. The post encourages more thoughtful and deliberate approaches to AI development and deployment.
